title: silky floral polish casserole
description: Cozy polish dish featuring parmesan and deglazed fond in a silky sauce. This recipe is shojin-friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and grease a 9-inch round baking dish.
In a medium saucepan, combine 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter and 1/4 cup of all-purpose flour. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture forms a smooth paste.
Gradually add in 1 cup of vegetable broth, whisking continuously, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add in 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 tablespoon of Dijon mustard, whisking until smooth, then remove from heat and set aside.
In a separate saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium heat. Add in 1 cup of mixed mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add in 2 cloves of minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add in 2 cups of spinach leaves and cook until wilted, about 1-2 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together 6 eggs, 1 cup of grated parmesan cheese, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Add in the cooked mushroom and spinach mixture, as well as the deglazed sauce, and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish and smooth the top.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the edges are set and the center is just slightly jiggly.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve warm, garnished with additional parmesan cheese and chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Slice the casserole into wedges and transfer to a lazy susan spinner for serving.
